vocabulary.meaning: to agree that something is true, especially unwillingly
vocabulary.example: He admitted that he had made a mistake.

vocabulary.meaning: to catch someone and take them to a police station because they are believed to have committed a crime
vocabulary.example: The police arrested the suspect near the scene of the robbery.

vocabulary.meaning: an organization set up to provide help and raise money for those in need
vocabulary.example: She donated all her old books to a local charity.

vocabulary.meaning: to do something illegal or perform a crime
vocabulary.example: It is a serious offence to commit a crime in this country.

vocabulary.meaning: a group of people living in the same place or having a particular characteristic in common
vocabulary.example: The local community came together to clean up the park.

vocabulary.meaning: a place where legal trials take place and law cases are decided
vocabulary.example: The witness had to appear in court to give evidence.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who has committed a crime; or relating to crime
vocabulary.example: The police are searching for the criminal who robbed the bank. / Criminal behaviour will not be tolerated.

vocabulary.meaning: the ideas, customs, and social behaviour of a particular people or society
vocabulary.example: We learned a lot about Japanese culture during our trip.

vocabulary.meaning: well known from long or close association
vocabulary.example: The town looked familiar, even though I hadn't been there in years.

vocabulary.meaning: the group of people with the authority to govern a country or state
vocabulary.example: The government announced new plans to improve public health services.

vocabulary.meaning: something that you do often and regularly, sometimes without knowing you are doing it
vocabulary.example: Biting your fingernails is a bad habit that is hard to break.

vocabulary.meaning: a card that carries official information about a person, such as their name and photo
vocabulary.example: You must show your identity card to enter the secure building.

vocabulary.meaning: contrary to or forbidden by law
vocabulary.example: It is illegal to drive a car without a valid license.

vocabulary.meaning: the activities associated with the governance of a country or area
vocabulary.example: He is very interested in international politics and current affairs.

vocabulary.meaning: all the inhabitants of a particular place, country, or area
vocabulary.example: The world population has increased rapidly over the last century.

vocabulary.meaning: a building where criminals are kept as a punishment
vocabulary.example: The thief was sent to prison for his crimes.

vocabulary.meaning: to express strong disapproval or objection; or an organized public demonstration of objection
vocabulary.example: Students protested against the tuition fee increase. / The peaceful protest took place in the main square.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who lives in a particular place
vocabulary.example: The residents of the street complained about the loud noise.

vocabulary.meaning: having an obligation to do something, or having control over or care for someone
vocabulary.example: Parents are responsible for the safety of their children.

vocabulary.meaning: to take money or property from a person or place illegally by using force or threats
vocabulary.example: Two masked men tried to rob the local grocery store.

vocabulary.meaning: a sequence of actions regularly followed; or performed as part of a regular procedure
vocabulary.example: Exercise is part of my daily routine. / The doctor performed a routine checkup.

vocabulary.meaning: a plan that gives expected times for different things to happen
vocabulary.example: We need to stick to our tight schedule to finish on time.

vocabulary.meaning: a set of circumstances in which one finds oneself
vocabulary.example: The emergency services managed to handle the difficult situation.

vocabulary.meaning: relating to society or its organization
vocabulary.example: Loneliness is a growing social problem in modern cities.

vocabulary.meaning: a large group of people who live together in an organized way
vocabulary.example: We live in a multicultural society with diverse traditions.

vocabulary.meaning: to take something that belongs to someone else without permission
vocabulary.example: Someone tried to steal my bicycle from outside the shop.

vocabulary.meaning: a custom or belief that has existed for a long time
vocabulary.example: It is a family tradition to eat dinner together on Sundays.

vocabulary.meaning: showing all the characteristics that you would expect from the particular person, thing, or group
vocabulary.example: A typical working day for her starts at 8:00 a.m.

vocabulary.meaning: to express a choice in an election, or an official choice made in an election
vocabulary.example: Citizens will vote for the new president next month. / The proposal was passed by a majority vote.

vocabulary.meaning: a place where young people can meet and take part in activities
vocabulary.example: He plays table tennis at the local youth club every Friday.

vocabulary.meaning: the thin hard areas that cover the upper surface of the ends of the fingers
vocabulary.example: She uses a file to keep her fingernails neat and clean.

vocabulary.meaning: very unpleasant, bad, or shocking
vocabulary.example: There was a horrible smell coming from the kitchen.

vocabulary.meaning: based on the principles of democracy; or relating to the Democratic Party
vocabulary.example: The country held its first free and democratic elections.

vocabulary.meaning: the group of people who are elected to make and change the laws of a country
vocabulary.example: The new law is currently being debated in parliament.

vocabulary.meaning: the crime of stealing from a place or person, especially using force
vocabulary.example: The police arrived within minutes of the bank robbery.

vocabulary.meaning: a flat, round dish from which food is eaten
vocabulary.example: She served the cake on a beautiful ceramic plate.

vocabulary.meaning: in an impolite or offensive way
vocabulary.example: He rudely interrupted her while she was speaking.

vocabulary.meaning: not pleasant; disagreeable, bad, or offensive
vocabulary.example: The weather was cold and unpleasant.

vocabulary.meaning: to make someone suffer for a crime or bad behaviour
vocabulary.example: The teacher punished the student for cheating on the exam.

vocabulary.meaning: responsible for a specified wrongdoing or crime
vocabulary.example: The jury found the defendant guilty of theft.

vocabulary.meaning: to spend time in a place waiting or doing nothing
vocabulary.example: Teenagers often hang around the shopping mall after school.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to enter a building by force, especially to steal something
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: A thief tried to break into the house while we were asleep.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to reach the same level or speed as someone who is ahead of you
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She skipped many classes, so she had to study hard to catch up with her friends.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to escape punishment for a wrong or illegal act
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The criminal managed to get away with the robbery because there were no witnesses.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to rise from bed or a sitting position
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: What time do you usually get up on weekends?

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to start living in a new house or flat
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We are planning to move in to our new apartment next Tuesday.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to return something to its correct place
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Please put away your toys after you finish playing.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to stop sleeping and open your eyes
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The alarm goes off at 6 a.m., but I rarely wake up immediately.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to wash the plates, cups, and cutlery after a meal
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Whose turn is it to wash up after dinner tonight?

prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: Stealing or vandalizing public property is strictly against the law.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: at a specific age or point of life
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: He learned to ride a bicycle at the age of five.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: in a place where other people can see or hear you
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: It can be embarrassing to have an argument in public.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: as a reply or reaction to something
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: She wrote a letter to the newspaper in response to their article.
EX: I still keep in touch with my primary school friends.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: at the age period of 13-19, 20-29, etc.
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: Many people go to university in their early twenties.

wordFormation.meaning: agreement: a decision or arrangement between people / disagree: to have a different opinion
wordFormation.example: EX: They signed a trade agreement. / We disagree on almost everything.

wordFormation.meaning: belief: a strong feeling that something is true or exists / believe: to accept something as true / believable or unbelievable: easy or difficult to accept as true
wordFormation.example: EX: He has a strong belief in democracy. / I believe we can solve this problem. / The excuse he gave was not very believable.

wordFormation.meaning: courage: the ability to do something that frightens you; bravery / courageous: very brave
wordFormation.example: EX: It takes courage to stand up for what you believe in. / The firefighter made a courageous attempt to save the family.

wordFormation.meaning: elect: choose someone by voting / election: the process of electing a representative
wordFormation.example: EX: They elected a new mayor yesterday. / The presidential election takes place next year.

wordFormation.meaning: equality: the state of being equal, especially in status, rights, or opportunities / unequal: not equal in quantity, size, or value
wordFormation.example: EX: Women are fighting for equality in the workplace. / The two sides received unequal shares of the funding.

wordFormation.meaning: live: to remain alive / alive: living; not dead
wordFormation.example: EX: She wants to live in a peaceful country. / The doctors managed to keep the patient alive.

wordFormation.meaning: nationality: the status of belonging to a particular nation / national: relating to a nation; citizen / international: between two or more nations
wordFormation.example: EX: What is your nationality? / The national library is closed on Sundays. / Geneva is home to many international organizations.

wordFormation.meaning: peace: freedom from disturbance; quiet / peaceful or peacefully: quiet and calm; in a calm way
wordFormation.example: EX: The two countries are there at peace. / They live in a peaceful village in the countryside. / The conflict was resolved peacefully.

wordFormation.meaning: prisoner: a person kept in prison as a punishment
wordFormation.example: EX: The prisoner escaped from his cell last night.

wordFormation.meaning: shot: the act of firing a gun, or a hit / shooting: the act of firing a gun
wordFormation.example: EX: He heard a loud shot in the distance. / Police are investigating the shooting near the bank.

wordPatterns.meaning: showing strong annoyance or displeasure about a matter
wordPatterns.example: EX: She was angry with him about his constant lateness.

wordPatterns.meaning: responsible for a specified wrongdoing or crime
wordPatterns.example: EX: The jury found him guilty of robbery.

wordPatterns.meaning: to charge someone with a crime or wrongdoing
wordPatterns.example: EX: They accused her of stealing the watch.

wordPatterns.meaning: to hold someone responsible for something bad
wordPatterns.example: EX: You can't blame me for the bad weather.

wordPatterns.meaning: to assign the responsibility for something bad to someone/something
wordPatterns.example: EX: She blamed the mistake on a computer error.

wordPatterns.meaning: to express disapproval of someone because of their mistakes or faults
wordPatterns.example: EX: The teacher criticized the boy for not paying attention.

wordPatterns.meaning: to stop thinking about or remembering something
wordPatterns.example: EX: Don't forget about the meeting tomorrow morning.

wordPatterns.meaning: to stop feeling angry or resentful towards someone for their mistake
wordPatterns.example: EX: I can't forgive him for lying to me.

wordPatterns.meaning: to ask someone politely to go to an event
wordPatterns.example: EX: They invited us to their wedding party.

wordPatterns.meaning: to make someone suffer for their crime or bad behaviour
wordPatterns.example: EX: The driver was punished for speeding.

wordPatterns.meaning: to give a portion of something to others
wordPatterns.example: EX: I'm happy to share my lunch with you.

wordPatterns.meaning: to make a facial expression showing pleasure or amusement at someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: She smiled at the baby in the carriage.
